hazards.name: Hydrocarbon Release from Process Equipment
hazards.description: Uncontrolled release of hydrocarbons (oil, gas, or condensate) from process piping, vessels, or wellhead equipment on the offshore platform.
hazards.topEventDescription: Breach in the pressure envelope of process equipment resulting in hydrocarbon release to the platform or sea.

threats.name: Internal Corrosion
threats.description: CO2 or H2S corrosion causing wall thinning in pipework and vessels, particularly at bends, dead legs, and areas of low flow velocity.

barriers.name: Corrosion Management Programme
barriers.description: Risk-based inspection programme with ultrasonic thickness measurement at documented TMLs. Corrosion inhibitor injection with residual monitoring.

barriers.name: Material Selection and Corrosion Allowance
barriers.description: Design-phase material selection based on corrosion modelling. Minimum 3mm corrosion allowance on carbon steel systems. CRA materials for high-risk circuits.

threats.name: Mechanical Seal or Gasket Failure
threats.description: Failure of flange gaskets, valve packing, or rotating equipment seals due to fatigue, thermal cycling, or incorrect installation.

barriers.name: Competency-Assured Joint Integrity
barriers.description: All bolted joints made up by competency-assessed technicians using calibrated torque equipment. Joint register with 100% traceability.

threats.name: Overpressure Event
threats.description: Process pressure exceeds design limits due to blocked outlet, thermal expansion, or upstream well control event.

barriers.name: Pressure Safety Valves (PSVs)
barriers.description: Independently verified PSVs sized per API 520/521, tested annually. Relief headers routed to flare system.

barriers.name: High-Integrity Pressure Protection System (HIPPS)
barriers.description: SIL-3 rated HIPPS on high-risk segments with automatic isolation on high-high pressure. Independent of the main control system.

consequences.name: Fire or Explosion
consequences.description: Ignition of released hydrocarbons resulting in jet fire, pool fire, or vapour cloud explosion on the platform.

barriers.name: Gas Detection and Emergency Shutdown
barriers.description: Fixed and portable gas detectors with automatic ESD activation at confirmed gas detection. Isolation of ignition sources within 3 seconds.

barriers.name: Active Fire Protection
barriers.description: Deluge system with firewater pumps (diesel and electric). Foam injection on helideck and process areas. Manual firefighting equipment.

barriers.name: Passive Fire Protection
barriers.description: PFP coatings on structural steel, firewalls between modules, and blast walls rated to defined overpressure scenarios.

consequences.name: Environmental Pollution
consequences.description: Hydrocarbon discharge to sea causing marine pollution, regulatory breach, and reputational damage.

barriers.name: Closed Drain System
barriers.description: All process areas drain to closed drain system with oil-water separation before any overboard discharge. Continuous oil-in-water monitoring.

barriers.name: Oil Spill Response Plan
barriers.description: Tier 1 response capability on-platform with dispersant, booms, and skimmers. Tier 2 mutual aid agreement with neighbouring operators.

consequences.name: Personnel Casualties
consequences.description: Injury or fatality to platform personnel from blast, thermal radiation, toxic gas exposure, or structural collapse.

barriers.name: Temporary Refuge and Evacuation
barriers.description: TR rated for defined blast and fire scenarios with HVAC positive pressure. Evacuation via TEMPSC lifeboats and helicopter. Muster drills weekly.

hazards.name: Dropped Object from Crane Operations
hazards.description: Loss of load during lifting operations using platform cranes, including personnel transfers, cargo lifts, and equipment handling.
hazards.topEventDescription: Load falls from crane during lifting due to rigging failure, mechanical failure, or operator error.

threats.name: Rigging Failure
threats.description: Sling, shackle, or rigging hardware fails under load due to overloading, damage, or exceeding SWL.

barriers.name: Lifting Equipment Inspection and Certification
barriers.description: All lifting equipment colour-coded quarterly, certified annually by competent person. Damaged equipment immediately quarantined.

barriers.name: Lift Planning and Permit to Work
barriers.description: All lifts require approved lift plan with load calculations, rigging diagram, and exclusion zone. Critical lifts require independent review.

consequences.name: Struck-By Injury or Fatality
consequences.description: Personnel struck by falling load or debris in the drop zone or swing radius.

barriers.name: Exclusion Zones and Banksman Control
barriers.description: Physical barricading of drop zone. Dedicated banksman with stop-work authority. No personnel under suspended loads.

consequences.name: Damage to Safety-Critical Equipment
consequences.description: Dropped load impacts process equipment, pipework, or safety systems causing secondary loss of containment.

barriers.name: Protection of Safety-Critical Equipment
barriers.description: Steel guards over critical pipework in crane operating radius. No-fly zones over live process areas during heavy lifts.
